observations.managementAction:
The report prepared did not include all properties.
The Mayor assign additional staff to assist the Municipal Treasurer in the preparation of the PCs;
The Acting Accountant maintain PPELCs for all PPE; PPELCs not complete.
Report not in prescribed form.
Preparation of property cards are on-going.
The Inventory Committee was already created. Likewise, the assigned job orders for the inventory just finished the tagging of current property per office. MTO and Accounting to consolidate available records to be used in the one-time cleansing.
observations.reason:
Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2013.
Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Observation (Obs.) No. 8.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
• Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of the required reconciliation between actual amounts reflected in the SCBAA and those presented in the SFPer under the General Fund, and include the said reconciliation in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements; and
• Ensure timely submission of the complete set of financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The reconciliation was not prepared.
There was delay in the submission of complete set of financial statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant perform reconciliation between the Surplus/(Deficit) in the SFPer and the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities in the SCF, and include the said reconciliation as part of the SCF or in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 2 and COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: No reconciliation was prepared.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 6.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management fill up the three mandatory positions under the LDRRMO and provide sufficient funds in order to optimize the performance of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Human Resource Management Office posted the said vacancy several times but, no avail since the applicants cannot pass the qualification standards.
observations.reason: Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
• Implement alternative procedures to gather relevant information and evidence to support the reported prior years’ balances such as, but not limited to, obtaining third-party confirmations in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers; and
• Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the limitations and uncertainties associated with the prior years’ balances.
observations.managementAction:
Not all prior years’ balances were supported with documents.
Not all prior years’ balances were provided with disclosures.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 11.

observations.managementAction:
Stockroom not provided.
Perpetual inventory method was not applied for all types of inventories.
SLCs were not complete
Physical count was conducted but details of the inventories were not complete.
RIS are prepared but was not consolidated into the SSMI
SC were not prepared on all types of inventories.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin all the department heads concerned to submit immediately the reports and documents requested by the Audit Team in line with the audit of accounts and transactions for CY 2021; and ensure timely submission of the year-end FS and its supporting documents in accordance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.
observations.managementAction: Enjoin all the department heads concerned to submit immediately the reports and documents requested by the Audit Team in line with the audit of accounts and transactions for CY 2021; and Ensure timely submission of the year-end FS and its supporting documents in accordance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.
observations.reason:
The complete set of FS was not submitted.
Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: There were delays incurred in the submission of the reports.
observations.reason:
Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2016.
Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 13.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
• Strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
• Refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with existing unliquidated cash advance; and
• Require the immediate liquidation of cash advances upon completion of its purpose.
observations.managementAction:
There was an instance of granting additional cash advances to an AO with unliquidated cash advance
Delayed liquidation of some cash advances due to delayed claims of beneficiaries.
Discontinue the practice of granting several cash advances nearing the last working day of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and submit the BRS within the prescribed period to facilitate verification of the cash in bank account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: There were still delays in the submission of the BRS.
observations.reason: Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer undertake the issuance and renewal of Property Acknowledgement Receipts every three years and keep a copy on file.
observations.managementAction: Waiting for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts to be conducted before the renewal of the PAR.

Physical count of PPEs was conducted but did not include all properties from all departments.
No request was submitted.
Properties were still not inspected by COA TAS.
The Municipal Accountant was not yet able to draw a JEV.
observations.reason: Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office take charge of the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: Some land titles were still in process with the CENRO.
observations.reason:
Observation was first raised in AAR CY 2007
Observation No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned agency officials should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passed away, amounting to ₱48,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Heirs refuse to acknowledge and pay the receivables.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant coordinate with the concerned offices to gather sufficient
documentation and establish the appropriate basis for recording the
unrecognized Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) amounting to ₱7.851
million in accordance with IPSAS Nos. 1 and 17 and to recognize the
corresponding depreciation to ensure the fair presentation and accuracy of
the financial statements.

2.10. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant:
• Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the balance reported in
the CIP account and the corresponding PPE account; and
• Recognize the depreciation expense and the corresponding accumulated
depreciation of the affected PPE account.

3.9. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant:
a. Prepare and record the necessary journal entries to recognize the
unrecorded RPT Receivables amounting to ₱0.762 million to ensure the
fair presentation of the financial statements; and
b. Strictly comply with IPSAS No. 23 and the NGAS Manual by timely
setting up Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property
Tax Income at the beginning of each year, in order to uphold the
reliability of financial records and the proper implementation of
prescribed accounting standards.

4.10. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant ensure the timely transfer of unexpended balances of the current
year’s LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund at year-end in compliance with
Section 21 of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002, to prevent
misstatement of accounts and safeguard the funds exclusively for disaster
risk reduction and management activities.

5.9. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant:
• Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of the
required reconciliation between actual amounts reflected in the SCBAA
and those presented in the SFPer under the General Fund, and include
the said reconciliation in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial
Statements; and
• Ensure timely submission of the complete set of financial statements.

6.9. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant perform reconciliation between the Surplus/(Deficit) in the
SFPer and the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities in the SCF, and
include the said reconciliation as part of the SCF or in the disclosures on the
Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 2 and COA
Circular No. 2016-004.

7.6. We recommended and the Management agreed to cancel checks long
outstanding and revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus of the
fund and adjust its books for fair presentation of Cash in Bank, Accounts
Payable and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements
pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume 1.

8.11. We recommended and the Management agreed that:
• The Inventory Committee and the Municipal Treasurer include all types
of properties from all offices in the physical count of PPE, submit the
RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the
Acting Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with
the accounting records;
• The Acting Municipal Accountant prepare subsidiary ledgers and
supporting documentation for all PPEs that were recorded as a group;
and
• The Municipality undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances of government agencies as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

9.17. We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
• The Acting Municipal Accountant implement the perpetual inventory
method, use the SSMI as basis for JEV preparation to recognize the
issuances of accountable forms expenses, and maintain SLC for
accountable forms; and
• The Municipal Treasurer summarize the RIS, prepare and submit the
SSMI to the Accountant to serve as basis in accounting for the issuances
of accountable forms.

10.9. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant:
• Continue to undertake measures to gather sufficient and reliable
information that will enable the reconstruction of subsidiary ledgers
supporting the ₱111.022 million balance under the PPE; and
• Include proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to
acknowledge the limitations and uncertainties related to the
unsubstantiated and unreconciled portion of the PPE.

11.7. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal
Accountant:
• Continue the reconciliation of the GL and SLs for the Due to National
Government Agencies account and prepare the necessary adjusting
entries, if warranted, in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume II, to ensure the reliability and accuracy of the
financial statements.; and
• Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the
limitations and uncertainties associated with the unreconciled balance.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
• Review the computation of depreciation and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17; and
• Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that:
• The LDRRM Officer maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities in order to attain the intended DRRM objectives of RA No. 10121; and
• The Management ensure that right-of-way for the project have been secured prior to the provision of allocation for future projects.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
• Make the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the net income earned, represented by the government equity account, in the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
• Accrue to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the income earned in the Trust Fund and subsequently transfer the respective amount to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
• Monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances from accountable officers as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and
• Submit the quarterly reports on the status of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period as required under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that:
• The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
• The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management submit the duly accomplished audit observations and recommendations within 60 days from receipt of the AAR to the Office of the Auditor. Moreover, that all items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that the Municipality:
• Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following the timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; and
• Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
We recommended that Management fully comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2018-61 to ensure that funds are only utilized on projects which was previously approved/intended.
observations.managementAction:
DILG demanded payment but the amount was still unreturned to date.
With pending court case.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.
observations.managementAction:
Based on the Status of Implementation of Prior Years’ Audit Recommendations in AAR CY 2013, the management submitted a partial report in Calendar Year 2007, however, requirements were not complied.
The Audit Team has not received any additional documents from the said NGO/PO.
The case was dismissed in the Office of the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant:

Implement alternative procedures to gather relevant information and evidence to support the reported prior years’ balances such as, but not limited to, obtaining third-party confirmations in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers; and

Provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements regarding the limitations and uncertainties associated with the prior years’ balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Enjoin all the department heads concerned to submit immediately the reports and documents requested by the Audit Team in line with the audit of accounts and transactions for CY 2021; and

Ensure timely submission of the year-end FS and its supporting documents in accordance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit and observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/ schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended the following:

Management fill up the three vacant mandatory positions under the LDRRMO in order to optimize the performance of the primary functions of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management; and

Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and submit the BRS within the prescribed period to facilitate verification of the cash in bank account of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality:

Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following the timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; and

Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer undertake the issuance and renewal of Property Acknowledgement Receipts every three years and keep a copy on file.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office take charge of the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.
We recommended that the agency officials concerned should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passed away, amounting to ₱48,000.00.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
Review the computation of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17; and
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of the required reconciliation between actual amounts reflected in the SCBAA and those presented in the SFPer under the General Fund, and include the said reconciliation in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements; and
Ensure timely submission of the complete set of financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant perform reconciliation between the Surplus/(Deficit) in the SFPer and the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities in the SCF, and include the said reconciliation as part of the SCF or in the disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 2 and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Management fill up the three mandatory positions under the LDRRMO and provide sufficient funds in order to optimize the performance of the primary functions of the LDRRMO and strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The LDRRM Officer maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities in order to attain the intended DRRM objectives of RA No. 10121; and
The Management ensure that right-of-way for the project have been secured prior to the provision of allocation for future projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the net income earned, represented by the government equity account, in the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
Accrue to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the income earned in the Trust Fund and subsequently transfer the respective amount to the General Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Monitor and demand timely submission of liquidation reports and/or immediate refund of any unused balances from accountable officers as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and
Submit the quarterly reports on the status of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period as required under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Management submit the duly accomplished AAPSI Form on the audit observations and recommendations within 60 days from receipt of the AAR to the Office of the Auditor. Moreover, all items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Acting Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and

The Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting entries to accurately reflect the balances of the PPE accounts and government equity in the financial statements:

Particulars   Debit  Credit

 Prior Period 
Adjustment	                                   xxx                                           
 Accumulated 
Depreciation           xxx
 Accumulated 
Impairment Loss 
(if applicable) xxx

        Account	       xxx
            To adjust for issued semi-expendable properties

        Prior Period 
        Adjustment    xxx
            To close the Prior Period Adjustment account

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Health Officer furnish copies of documents and reports pertaining to the receipt and utilization of COVID-19 vaccines to the Acting Municipal Accountant;
The Acting Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the receipt and utilization of COVID-19 vaccines by making the following entries:

	Drugs and 
   Medicines 
   Inventory    xxx
	     Grants and 
        Donations in 
        Kind                xxx

   Medicines

   pertains to CY)

   pertains 
   to PY)        xxx

       Medicines 
       Inventory	        xxx
	          To take up utilization of vaccines

The Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Provincial Health Office and the Department of Health to obtain the cost or fair value of the vaccines received by the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Health Officer refer to the DPRI of the DOH when setting up the unit prices of essential drugs and medicines in the Purchase Requests (PR) to ensure that reasonable prices are obtained by the Municipality; and

The BAC Chairperson utilize the DPRI as basis in the review of the submitted PRs for the procurement of essential drugs and medicines to ensure that the DPRI is used in setting up the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources – Designate initiate the process on the closure of operations of controlled dumpsites to ensure the attainment of objectives on ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of pre-signing checks without the supporting documents in order to maintain sound internal control and safeguard public funds from risks of loss, theft or misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collectors and other accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAF and submit it together with the CRAAF for proper monitoring in accordance with Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended the following:

Comply with the posting and reporting requirements on the fund utilization and status of program/project implementation of the LGSF-FA and LGSF-Other FA in accordance with DBM LBC Nos. 122 and 128; and

Submit the required reports prescribed under Sections 1.a.2 and 1.b.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 20, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.
We recommended that the agency officials concerned should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passed away, amounting to ₱48,000.00.
observations.reason: Heirs refuse to acknowledge and pay the receivables.  Write-off will be requested to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of results of the RPCPPE with the accounting records;
The Municipal Accountant see to it that all PPE accounts in the books are supported with PPE ledger cards; and
The designated property officer prepare and maintain the prescribed property cards per category of PPE indicating therein the relevant information for proper monitoring.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Acting Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, impose liquidated damages for infrastructure projects completed beyond the targeted completion date as stipulated in the contract; and
The Acting Municipal Accountant make sure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Acting Municipal Accountant make sure that disbursements for financial assistance are supported with complete documentation in accordance with DSWD MC No. 24 s. 2020 dated October 19, 2020; and
The MSWDO – Office Care Taker ensure that the individual certification that the client/beneficiary is in need of assistance, case summaries and case studies are duly prepared for each beneficiary and submitted to the Acting Municipal Accountant to support the disbursement of financial assistance.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert all necessary efforts to render the agency’s accounts within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer submit the monthly CRAAF as well as require all accountable officers to prepare and timely submit the monthly RAAFs for proper monitoring in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and submit the BRS within the prescribed period to facilitate verification of the cash in bank account of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality:
Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following the timetables set in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;
Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73;
Appropriate a separate budget for the operations of the MDRRMO from where administrative expenditures for personnel services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay be charged; and
Source the budgetary requirements of the Emergency Response Unit (ERU) and the Incident Command System (ICS) of the Municipality from the different offices found to have crucial roles in its operation and not solely from the LDRRMF. The budgetary share of the ERU and ICS from the LDRRMF should be strictly DRRM-related such as disaster operations center and early warning system, as defined in the regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances and submit a corrected Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and
The MDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in its prescribed form pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of the same Circular.
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
The Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
The Local Chief Executive ensure inclusion to their succeeding appropriations necessary expenditures, such as manpower requirements, operating expenses and information technology equipment, for the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and
The Municipality furnish the Audit Team of the CY 2020 GPB duly reviewed and endorsed by DILG.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the guidelines and procedures provided by DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 in order to timely validate compliance and facilitate monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the grant.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer undertake the issuance and renewal of Property Acknowledgement Receipts every three years and keep a copy on file.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer improve her monitoring and supervision functions and diligently update the information on the project billboard of all projects to enhance its transparency in matters of project implementation.
We recommended that Management fully comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2018-61 to ensure that funds are only utilized on projects which was previously approved/intended.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:
Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year;
Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date; and
Ensure that the fund is sourced appropriately.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Municipal Treasurer update his Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Cards and submit a certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the calendar year. The total amount in the certified list should be reconciled with the total amount per schedule furnished by the Municipal Assessor;

The Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to set up the receivable based on the duly certified list and ensure the proper recording of collection in the corresponding subsidiary ledger; and

The Municipal Accountant analyze the causes of the discrepancies between reciprocal accounts and draw the necessary JEVs to adjust their balances.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:

Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays;



Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and

Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that Management:

Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and

Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office take charge of the titling of all parcels of land of the Municipal Government;
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.
We recommended that the agency officials concerned should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passed away, amounting to ₱48,000.00.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct annual inventory taking for Property, Plant and Equipment;
Prepare a Report on Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment with complete data; and
Instruct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledger for PPE respectively, and ensure that records are reconciled.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to establish the book value of unserviceable properties.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:
Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays;
Carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and
Strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended that Management:
Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in the filling-up of permanent personnel in the MDRRMO; and
Allocate sufficient budget so that the Municipality can appoint permanent personnel in the MDRRMO.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit a schedule bearing the names of landowners with areas and assessed value of the lands they own to the Municipal Treasurer;

The Municipal Treasurer update his RPT Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Cards and submit a certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the calendar year.  The total amount in the certified list should be reconciled with the total amount per schedule furnished by the Municipal Assessor; and

The Municipal Accountant shall prepare a JEV to set up the receivable based on the duly certified list and ensure the proper recording of collection in the corresponding subsidiary ledger.

We recommended the following:

Management should thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that factors such as location and topographic conditions are considered prior to the approval of projects in order to minimize delays and realignment of projects;

Management should carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and

Management should strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and

The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every PR and PO in order to make sure that the goods procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users.  Likewise, ensure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the liquidation of petty cash within the prescribed period.

Require the accountable officer to immediately liquidate in full the cash advances on petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended the following:

Management should ensure that resources of the government are fully utilized to attain efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the government as well as deliver the intended benefits of the programs to the constituents of the Municipality.

Management should obtain a copy of the notarized MOA from BFAR RO VI in order to determine the conditions to be complied to transfer ownership of said boats to the Municipality.
We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the SEF Budget in accordance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017.

We recommended that the Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the NSWMC for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.

observations.reason: Waiting for the action of Legislative Department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency officials concerned should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passed away, amounting to ₱48,000.00.
observations.reason: Heirs refuse to acknowledge and pay the receivables.

We recommended the following:
Management should thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects and see to it that factors such as location and topographic conditions are considered prior to the approval of projects in order to minimize delays and realignment of projects;
Management should carefully plan their projects to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year; and
Management should strictly monitor the implementation of projects and see to it that projects are completed within the targeted completion date.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required financial reports and other supporting documents/schedules as set forth by existing rules and regulations; and
The concerned accountable officers should also look into the root cause of the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and take the necessary actions to ensure adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every PR and PO in order to make sure that the goods procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users.  Likewise, ensure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the liquidation of petty cash within the prescribed period.
Require the accountable officer to immediately liquidate in full the cash advances on petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements in the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with existing rules and regulations.  Send demand letters to officials and employees requiring liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended the following:
Management should ensure that resources of the government are fully utilized to attain efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the government as well as deliver the intended benefits of the programs to the constituents of the Municipality.
Management should obtain a copy of the notarized MOA from BFAR RO VI in order to determine the conditions to be complied to transfer ownership of said boats to the Municipality.
We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the SEF Budget in accordance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017.

We recommended that the Management hasten the revision of the 10-year Municipal SWMP and submit to the NSWMC for approval to ensure that the SWM programs and activities are planned and implemented with efficiency and effectiveness and aligned with the national government goals.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee tasked to conduct physical count of PPE strictly discharge its functions as stated in the EO No. 2014-028 to ascertain the correctness and existence of the PPE.
observations.managementAction: The management had already created an inventory team to conduct the inventory taking.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 1

Finding was first raised in AAR CY 2013, Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that 

(1) The Local Chief Executive strictly implement the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26,1977 particularly on the  preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation and attach the same to Disbursement Vouchers to support payments for fuel consumed, and 



(2) Disbursements for fuel consumption be duly supported with properly accomplished and approved serially numbered driver’s trip tickets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. 

Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were prepared but not submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required accounting and financial reports set forth by existing rules and regulations and implement adequate measures that will enable the Agency to meet such deadlines.
observations.managementAction: There were still reports that were not submitted within the set deadlines.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor the compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of MDRRMF pursuant to Section V.A.1 of COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted but not on a regular basis.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oversee the prompt implementation of the 20% development programs and projects in accordance with the planned schedule to avoid rising cost of materials and labor and to ensure attainment of the social and economic objectives of these projects.
observations.managementAction: There were still delays in the implementation of some projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal School Board formulate a budget strictly in accordance with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and related DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars to ensure that SEF is used only for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women, Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA CircularNo. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan support the construction of disaster resilient evacuation center and refrain from disapproving a portion or whole of the funding of the mandatory 5%  of the estimated revenue from regular sources as the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) as long as compliant to the requirements of RA 10121. The disapproval of the funding components of projects without sufficient valid reasons had sacrificed its implementation and deprived the targeted beneficiaries of their immediate benefits.

We recommended that management should exert efforts to follow-up the liquidation of the funds released.  

Strictly comply with the documentary requirements and conditions set forth in the COA Circular No. 2007-001 and adopt a system of monitoring and inspection of the projects being implemented by the NGOs/POs to ensure that the funds were actually utilized for the purpose for which they were granted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to process the titles of these properties in the name of the LGU and should be properly kept by the general services officer or the local treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Lands were only supported by Tax Declarations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant Designate, the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the Department of Agriculture funded projects should require the recipient NGO/PO – Women, Inc. of Belison to comply with the requirements of COA CircularNo. 96-003 to ascertain the propriety of expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The said NGO/PO were no longer in existence
Waiting for the action of Legislative Department

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to process the titles of these properties in the name of the LGU and should be properly kept by the general services officer or the local treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Non-existence of CENRO

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency officials concerned should exert efforts to collect from the surviving heirs receivables from officials who have already passes away, amounting to P48,000.00.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee tasked to conduct physical count of PPE strictly discharge its functions as stated in the EO No. 2014-028 to ascertain the correctness and existence of the PPE.

We recommended that (1) the Local Chief Executive strictly implement the provision of COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26,1977 particularly on the  preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, for audit examination and evaluation and attach the same  to Disbursement Vouchers to support payments for fuel consumed, and (2) disbursements for fuel consumption be duly supported with properly accomplished and approved serially numbered driver’s trip tickets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for control and monitoring purposes. Further, we recommended that constant follow up of bank statements be made with the depository bank for prompt preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal  General Services Officer and other accountable officers to observe the deadlines for the submission of required accounting and financial reports set forth by existing rules and regulations and implement adequate measures that will enable the Agency to meet such deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor the compliance with the reporting requirement on the sources and utilization of MDRRMF pursuant to Section V.A.1 of COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oversee the prompt implementation of the 20% development programs and projects in accordance with the planned schedule to avoid rising cost of materials and labor and to ensure attainment of the social and economic objectives of these projects.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board formulate a budget strictly in accordance with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and related DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars to ensure that SEF is used only for its intended purpose.
We recommended that the LGU officials observe the pertinent laws and regulations in the procurement/purchase of motor vehicle, and not to utilize the 20% development fund and LDRRMF for this purpose.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor strictly that activities are implemented on the targeted period of implementation so that expected benefits due to beneficiaries/constituents will be achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor prepare and submit a schedule bearing the names of landowners with areas and assessed value of the lands they own to the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Cards and submit a certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the calendar year.  The total amount in the certified list should be reconciled with the total amount per schedule furnished by the Municipal Assessor.
We recommended that the local sanggunian update its existing internal rules of procedures and prescribe performance standards of its members for the purpose of RATA computation.

We recommend that the inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE to be used as basis for booking up and reconciliation of PPE accounts. After reconciliation the Municipal Accountant effect necessary adjustments if any in the books of accounts for fairness of presentation of the PPE account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee were created this year

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer of Belison, Antique require collecting officers to prepare the Report of Collection and Deposits at the close of each business day and to remit their collections on a daily basis. To enforce this policy we recommend that the Local Chief Executive issue an executive order to this effect.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should conduct joint review of the accounting policies and procedures to strengthen controls with regards to collections and deposits.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the liquidation of these outstanding cash advances and require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor liquidation thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit a schedule bearing the names of landowners with areas including the assessed value of the lands they own to the Municipal Treasurer and the  Municipal Treasurer to update her Real Property Tax  Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Cards and submit a certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amounts to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the calendar year.  The total amount in the certified list should be reconciled with the total amount per schedule furnished by the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: Encoding on computer ledger of RPT taxpayer is still on going

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Annual Budget be passed and approved earlier during the year so that projects under the 20% Development Plan can be implemented on the targeted period and expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents which  can uplift their living conditions be delivered on time.
We recommend that appropriation for GAD Program be increased to five (5) percent of the LGU’s total appropriation and  all targets embodied in the Annual GAD Plan be implemented.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee should strictly discharge its functions as stated in the EO No. 2014-028 to ascertain the correctness, existence, ownership, valuation and encumbrances as well as propriety, of items composing the respective PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: No Inventory Committee was created

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan support the construction of disaster resilient evacuation center and refrain from disapproving a portion or whole of the funding of the mandatory 5%  of the estimated revenue from regular sources as the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) as long as compliant to the requirements of RA 10121. The disapproval of the funding components of projects without sufficient valid reasons had sacrificed its implementation and deprived the targeted beneficiaries of their immediate benefits.
observations.managementAction: The appropriation were re-program and procurement were made

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in charge of preparing the monthly payrolls to effect adjustments on deductions so that monthly net take home pay of all employees are not lower than the required minimum monthly net take home pay pursuant to the provisions of General Appropriations Act for ,he year. Likewise, inform the personnel concerned to make proper agreements with the lending institutions for the adjustments made especially on employees’ loan repayments.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require submission of attendance sheet, minutes of meetings of the SB Members as well as the Daily Time Record of the Department Heads and in the case of the LCE, accomplishment of work schedule for the month.
We also recommend checking of completeness of the required documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before effecting payment.
We recommend that the management follow the provisions of NDRRMC DILG DBM CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 particularly on the creation of an Office together with its mandatory positions.

We recommend that management appropriate the 20% IRA to development projects in accordance with the provisions of Sec. 287 of Ra 7160 and the DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to ensure that the targeted beneficiaries could avail of the immediate benefits from such projects.
We also recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality support the implementation of the Projects identified in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality and submit a duly accomplished report thereon to determine existence and condition of the agency’s assets.  Also, direct the Municipal Treasurer, who is the designated Property Custodian to prepare property cards and reconcile the same with the accounting records.  In the absence of records, create an appraisal committee to determine the appraised value of the assets as basis for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Due to non-creation of Inventory Committee

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should exert efforts to follow-up the liquidation of the funds released.  Strictly comply with the documentary requirements and conditions set forth in the COA Circular No. 2007-001 and adopt a system of monitoring and inspection of the projects being implemented by the NGOs/POs to ensure that the funds were actually utilized for the purpose for which they were granted.
observations.managementAction: The claimants could not be located

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.
